posts.text: Today's News Live Show 3/16/2026 nitter.net/i/broadcasts/1nxeLybkO…

Yes, we were all Mossad plants, even @lhfang


Besim (@besimistic)

Looking back now, it's clear that the Twitter Files was clearly a Mossad operation to hide their central involvement in Twitter censorship.

Why else was it a limited release with only Zios assets like Weiss, Taibbi, and Shellenberger given access?



— https://nitter.net/besimistic/status/2032635120255893982#m

New Podcast: Today's News, With @mtracey . Episode One, "Conspiretardation."

A message in a bottle, from a world gone crazy

racket.news/publish/post/190…

Yes, deep ties to Bill Gates, Larry Summers, Ehud Barak, Deepak Chopra, Ron Lauder, and Woody Allen convinced me to cover up their participation in a secret child buggering-and-eating cabal bent on starting World War III. My college president Leon Botstein recruited me.


ಠ_ಠ (@dudeguylive)

Lmao this coming from Matt “I’m a big tough journalist that’s suddenly quiet on Epstein because I dearly support many involved” Taibbi 

Real rich of you to get all sassy with this guy because you know he’s

This is a lie. I started covering digital censorship in 2018, in a feature story in Rolling Stone
rollingstone.com/politics/po…
I tried for a while to get others in the MSM interested in the topic, failed, then when I moved to Substack in early 2019 made censorship of both left and right a primary focus. I wrote years of reports, most under the "Meet the Censored" rubric. Once Musk came forward in late 2022 all I did was cover the exact same topic, just with inside material. The first Twitter Fi

That I agree with.


Thorkil_Heldum (@Thorkil_Heldum)

The interesting aspect the story is the media induced mass formation psychosis and moral panic. It's like Covid hysteria + Russiagate + QAnon with a force multiplier of both sides being equally invested in accusing each other. That is will be studied for decades.



— https://nitter.net/Thorkil_Heldum/status/2031757472340287660#m

You repost material by Malcolm Nance, who might be most consistently wrong person on this platform. I seriously doubt you were ever a fan of mine.


Summer (@Summatimesail)

I guess we'll have to see what they present in the case.  I am disillusioned by your perspective after I became such a fan of your hard work on the financial system. It sucks to lose trust in voices who understand how to research and present "the case". I will keep reading...



— https://nitter.net/Summatimesail/status/2031

If you're referring to me, I have no "patrons" of any kind.


Whitney Webb (@_whitneywebb)

take a look a the trajectory of the biggest journalists involved in the twitter files over the past 12-24 months.



— https://nitter.net/_whitneywebb/status/2031744938640068782#m
Leaving aside that there's a specific legislative carve-out exempting "informational materials" from IEEPA sanctions, it's beyond asinine to charge someone criminally for being a TV host.

This isn't hard. Technically he was charged with providing services to a sanctioned company. What services? "Hosting and producing" a television program. It's on the first page of the indictment.




Just Human (@realjusthuman)

Huh. Strange. 

I don’t see “crime of hosting a TV program” among the actual charges against Simes.







— https://nitter.net/realjusthuman/status/2031722541690044852#m

Simes has been a public figure in America since the Nixon years. Every foreign correspondent for decades quoted him in Russia/USSR stories. There’s no allegation of anything intelligence related here - just work hosting a TV program. Are we prosecuting imagined offenses?


Summer (@Summatimesail)

To represent that this dude was some type of journalist or pundit and targeted is disingenuous. This looks like tax fraud for mobsters…prosecute for sanctions violations because the rest is that murky

This is like the tenth year I’ve had people complain “Nothing else to write about?” when I express skepticism about current thing manias that everyone is writing about from another direction


The Hun 🇺🇸 (@thepatriotx21)

Who gives a shit?  Nothing else to post about?



— https://nitter.net/thepatriotx21/status/2031505206887006391#m

I’d suggest waiting until I defend an actual pedophile, but be you


. (@ovox__)

Alright you’ve shown your true color Matt. Can’t follow a pedo defender. Gross!



— https://nitter.net/ovox__/status/2031502717441470860#m

Thank you, @grok!


Grok (@grok)

No. Matt Taibbi is quoting an official 1992 FBI report by agent Kenneth Lanning ("Investigator's Guide to Allegations of 'Ritual' Child Abuse"). It examined claims of organized satanic cults committing mass child murders, sacrifices, and abuse involving "tens of thousands" of victims—and found no corroborative evidence: no bodies, no physical traces, no confirmed cults despite investigations and digs. 

The report urges evidence-based probes over hype. That's st

How is it not an injustice? Under this logic any employee of a state news agency can be a criminal.


Matt (@801formal_llama)

From what I can tell it doesn't look like an injustice.



— https://nitter.net/801formal_llama/status/2031497198844985462#m

Remember how people believed “tens of thousands” of murders had been committed? Good times ojp.gov/pdffiles1/Digitizati…


Lou Dax (@UpstandingMembr)

"fbi went underground they found what the kids described"





— https://nitter.net/UpstandingMembr/status/2031499094666182796#m
Will Evicting Blackstone Help Young People Buy Homes? (by Eric Salzman). Mega investors are a mega problem. But not the only one.
